passages.title: Answers Underground
passages.source_html:
Answers Underground


Burying greenhouse gases to slow global warming


A


One way to slow global warming is to take the greenhouse gases that cause it and bury them. That is the idea behind projects now under way to capture emissions from power plants and factories and force them underground or deep into the ocean. There, proponents argue, they could be trapped for thousands of years.


B


This concept, known as carbon sequestration, is already being used by oil companies to improve the efficiency of oil wells, and now engineers have begun exploring ways to capture carbon dioxide emissions from power plants to reduce their impact on the environment. At a recent conference, delegates from fourteen industrialised and developing countries agreed to engage in cooperative research into capturing and storing carbon dioxide.


C


The goal is to stabilise emissions of greenhouse gases that trap heat in the atmosphere. Over the past century, airborne carbon dioxide concentrations have risen by nearly a third, according to Scott Klara, sequestration manager at the US National Energy Technology Laboratory. Unless emissions are slashed by two thirds worldwide, the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change predicts that concentrations will rise to double the levels of the early 1700s, before the Industrial Revolution. These increased levels of carbon-based compounds in the atmosphere are believed to be the cause of rising temperatures and sea levels around the world. Ignoring the problem is therefore not an option.


D


Limiting emissions, however, is not an easy undertaking since increased energy consumption is a key to economic growth. Two thirds of the world's power-generating capacity, expected to come into use by 2030, has not been constructed yet, according to the International Energy Agency. The developing world will be particularly important. China and India alone are expected to account for two thirds of the global increase in coal usage over the next fifteen years.

E

Solutions are being sought. Work is being undertaken with alternatives to fossil fuels such as wind and solar energy, but it will be a long time before these alternative sources play a major role in fulfilling the world's energy needs. Geophysicist Klaus Lackner points out that around 85% of the world's energy is derived from fossil fuels, the cheapest and most plentiful energy source available, and the developing world in particular is unlikely to give them up. That is why many scientists support sequestration.


F


However, several problems must be resolved before sequestration plays a key role in a low-carbon future. One is the cost of capturing carbon dioxide. A second is storing the gas safely once it's been captured. Today, it costs about $US50 to extract and store a tonne of carbon dioxide from a power plant, which raises the cost of producing electricity by 30-80%. Lackner argues that it is too expensive to adapt existing plants to capture carbon dioxide. Instead, he recommends that carbon-capturing capacity be built into future plants. Economic incentives are needed to encourage companies to identify low-cost carbon-sequestration solutions. A government-supported program in the US has enabled some factories to partially capture carbon emissions, which they then sell for various uses, including carbonating soft drinks. However, there are no power plants ready for full carbon capture.


G


Once the carbon has been captured it must be stored. Natural carbon sinks, such as forests and wetlands, can remove some carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, but not nearly enough. Carbon dioxide could be pumped to the bottom of the ocean, where the pressure would keep it pinned to the seabed in liquid form for decades, but that has serious long-term environmental risks. David Hawkins, from the Natural Resources Defense Council in Washington, warns that the carbon dioxide could radically alter the chemical balance in the ocean, with potentially harmful consequences for marine life. Others worry that the carbon dioxide could escape back into the atmosphere.


H


A few promising attempts at underground carbon sequestration are currently under way. In western Canada, an oil company is pumping liquefied carbon dioxide into oil wells to force more oil to the surface and boost recovery by 10-15%. The company gets the carbon dioxide via a pipeline from North Dakota in the US, where the gas is captured from a synthetic-fuel plant. In another instance in the North Sea, a Norwegian energy firm is injecting carbon dioxide waste from its natural-gas operations into a saline aquifer 1,000 metres beneath the ocean floor.


I


Clearly, storing large amounts of gas underground raises environmental fears. Environmentalists argue that more research is needed on potential storage sites, such as oil and gas reservoirs and coal seams unsuitable for mining, to ensure that they offer long-term solutions. The World Wide Fund for Nature Australia has argued that the primary risk of underground storage is that dangerously large volumes of carbon dioxide might escape and people become asphyxiated.


J


Little progress in slashing global greenhouse gases can be achieved without involving developing countries, but for now carbon sequestration is not their priority because of the increased costs this would add to energy production. Hawkins argues that, to encourage developing nations to use sequestration, developed nations will have to provide assistance. He suggests a multilateral initiative in which developed nations, perhaps by purchasing carbon credits from poorer countries, finance the difference between the cost of a regular coal-fired power plant and one that captures carbon emissions. That is, the rich – who will remain the world's biggest polluters for years to come – would buy the right to emit carbon from the poor, who would use the proceeds to build better plants.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1– 6


Choose the correct people and organisation (A – F) for each issue. You may choose any correct person ororganisation more than once.


List of People and Organisations

A       Scott Klara

B       Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change

C       International Energy Agency

D       Klaus Lackner

E       David Hawkins

F       World Wide Fund for Nature Australia

Options (Word Bank):
A. A Scott Klara
B. B Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change
C. C International Energy Agency
D. D Klaus Lackner
E. E David Hawkins
F. F World Wide Fund for Nature Australia
Question / ParagraphABCDEF
1

1 the cost implications of fitting plants with the necessary equipment
✅ 正确答案：D

定位关键词：

cost implications；plants；equipment


原文：

F段，“Lackner argues that it is too expensive to adapt existing plants to capture carbon dioxide. Instead, he recommends that carbon-capturing capacity be built into future plants.  ”


解析：

原文中Klaus Lackner拉克纳认为，改造现有的工厂（plant）来捕获二氧化碳太昂贵了，相反，他建议在未来的工厂中建立碳捕获能力。体现了为工厂配备必要设备的成本影响。


同义替换：

adapt existing plants=fitting plants
2

2 the effects of sequestration could have on sea creatures
✅ 正确答案：E

定位关键词：

sequestration；sea creatures


原文：

G段，“David Hawkins, from the Natural Resources Defense Council in Washington, warns that the carbon dioxide could radically alter the chemical balance in the ocean, with potentially harmful consequences for marine life.”


解析：

原文中David Hawkins认为二氧化碳可能会从根本上改变海洋中的化学平衡，对海洋生物造成潜在的有害后果，与第二题相符。


同义替换：

marine life=sea creatures
3

3 the reasons why products such as oil and gas continue to be popular energy sources
✅ 正确答案：D

定位关键词：

growing economy；power


原文：

E段，“Geophysicist Klaus Lackner points out that around 85% of the world's energy is derived from fossil fuels, the cheapest and most plentiful energy source available, and the developing world in particular is unlikely to give them up. That is why many scientists support sequestration.”


解析：

原文中Klaus Lackner指出许多国家继续使用化石燃料的理由是世界上大约85%的能源来自化石燃料，这是最便宜、最丰富的能源。


同义替换：

fossil fuels=oil and gas
4

4 the need for industrialised countries to give aid to less wealthy countries
✅ 正确答案：E

定位关键词：

industrialised countries；less wealthy countries


原文：

J段，“Hawkins argues that, to encourage developing nations to use sequestration, developed nations will have to provide assistance.”


解析：

原文中Hawkins认为，为了鼓励发展中国家使用碳封存，发达国家必须提供援助。


同义替换：

industrialised countries=developed nations, less wealthy countries=developing nations
5

5 the significant increase in carbon dioxide concentrations in the air over the last 100 years
✅ 正确答案：A

定位关键词：

carbon dioxide concentrations；air；last 100 years


原文：

C段，“The goal is to stabilise emissions of greenhouse gases that trap heat in the atmosphere. Over the past century, airborne carbon dioxide concentrations have risen by nearly a third, according to Scott Klara, sequestration manager at the US National Energy Technology Laboratory.”


解析：

原文中Scott Klara表示，在过去一个世纪里，空气中的二氧化碳浓度上升了近三分之一。


同义替换：

in the air=in the atmosphere, past century=last 100 years
6

6 the potential for sequestration to harm human life
✅ 正确答案：F

定位关键词：

harm human life


原文：

I段，“The World Wide Fund for Nature Australia has argued that the primary risk of underground storage is that dangerously large volumes of carbon dioxide might escape and people become asphyxiated.”


解析：

原文中澳大利亚世界自然基金会(World Wide Fund for Nature Australia)认为，地下储存的主要风险是大量二氧化碳可能会泄漏出去，造成人们窒息（asphyxiated），即危害人类生命。

Questions 7 - 9


Look at the ten paragraphs (A – J). Which paragraph contains the following information?

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
7

7 examples of sequestration already in use in several parts of the world
✅ 正确答案：H

定位关键词：

examples of sequestration；several parts of the world


原文：

H段，“A few promising attempts at underground carbon sequestration are currently under way. In western Canada, an oil company is pumping liquefied carbon dioxide into oil wells to force more oil to the surface and boost recovery by 10-15%. The company gets the carbon dioxide via a pipeline from North Dakota in the US, where the gas is captured from a synthetic-fuel plant. In another instance in the North Sea, a Norwegian energy firm is injecting carbon dioxide waste from its natural-gas operations into a saline aquifer 1,000 metres beneath the ocean floor.”


解析：

原文例举了加拿大西部、北海等地的公司进行的地下碳封存的各项尝试。
8

8 an example of putting carbon dioxide emissions to use in the food and beverage industry
✅ 正确答案：F

定位关键词：

carbon dioxide emissions；food and beverage industry


原文：

F段，“A government-supported program in the US has enabled some factories to partially capture carbon emissions, which they then sell for various uses, including carbonating soft drinks.”


解析：

原文中美国政府支持的一项计划使一些工厂能够部分捕获碳排放，然后将其出售用于各种用途，包括碳酸软饮料，即用于食品和饮料行业（food and beverage industry）
9

9 current examples of the environmental harm attributed to carbon dioxide in the air
✅ 正确答案：C

审题：题目问当前二氧化碳对环境造成的危害实例。


定位关键信息：“environmental harm”“carbon dioxide in the air”。


对应原文：C段，“increased levels of carbon-based compounds…are believed to be the cause of rising temperatures and sea levels around the world”。


解题思路：C段提到二氧化碳浓度上升导致全球气温和海平面上升，说明了当前二氧化碳对环境的危害，因此答案为C段。


同义替换：题目中的“environmental harm”替换为“rising temperatures and sea levels”。

Questions 10 - 13


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

10

Both developing and developed nations have decided to investigate carbon dioxide sequestration.

正确答案： A. TRUE

定位关键词：

developing and developed nations；carbon dioxide sequestration


原文：

B段，“At a recent conference, delegates from fourteen industrialised and developing countries agreed to engage in cooperative research into capturing and storing carbon dioxide.”


解析：

原文提到在最近的一次会议上，来自14个工业化国家和发展中国家的代表同意就捕获和储存二氧化碳进行合作研究，与题目信息一致。


同义替换：

Industrialised country =developed country, carbon dioxide sequestration=capturing and storing carbon dioxide

11

A growing economy will use more power.

正确答案： A. TRUE

定位关键词：

growing economy；power


原文：

D段，“Limiting emissions, however, is not an easy undertaking since increased energy consumption is a key to economic growth.”


解析：

原文中指出能源消耗的增加是经济增长的关键。题目与原文完全符合。



同义替换：

use more power=increased energy consumption, economic growth=growing economy

12

Capturing carbon dioxide has become financially attractive.

正确答案： B. FALSE

定位关键词：

capturing carbon dioxide


原文：

F段，“However, several problems must be resolved before sequestration plays a key role in a low-carbon future. One is the cost of capturing carbon dioxide.”



解析：

原文中提到在碳封存在低碳未来发挥关键作用之前，必须解决几个问题，其一是捕获二氧化碳的成本，表明捕获二氧化碳的成本是很大的，而题目说的是捕获二氧化碳在经济上具有吸引力，表明成本低，两者矛盾，所以false。

13

More forests need to be planted to improve the atmosphere.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

审题：题目问是否需要种更多的树来改善大气


定位关键信息：forest, atmosphere


原文：G段，Once the carbon has been captured it must be stored. Natural carbon sinks, such as forests and wetlands, can remove some carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, but not nearly enough. 


解析：原文只提到种树可以减少大气中的二氧化碳，并未提到需要种植更多的森林。
